metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the generated email includes all required key facts accurately and naturally.

rubric.description: The email ignores the required facts or contradicts them.
Only a tiny fragment of one required fact appears.
Some facts are present, but multiple important facts are missing or distorted.
Most facts are present, but one important fact is missing, weakened, or ambiguous.
Almost all required facts are present, with only a minor omission or awkward compression.
Every required fact is included accurately and naturally, with no unsupported factual claims.

metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the generated email matches the requested tone in context.

rubric.description: The tone is absent, opposite of the requested tone, or unusable for the context.
The tone is very far from the requested tone.
The tone only loosely resembles the requested tone and is noticeably mismatched.
The tone is partly aligned but generic, uneven, or inconsistent.
The tone is mostly aligned with only small mismatches.
The tone closely and consistently matches the requested tone throughout.

metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the output works as a professional email.

rubric.description: The output is not usable as an email.
It barely resembles a professional email or collapses greeting, body, closing, and signature into a flat single paragraph.
Some email elements are present, but structure is incomplete, hard to scan, or mostly unformatted.
Basic email content is present, but paragraphing, greeting, body, or sign-off formatting is noticeably weak.
The email is clear and professional with only minor structure or formatting rough edges.
The subject and body are polished, clear, concise, and formatted with distinct greeting, body paragraphing, and sign-off.

metrics.reason: The email includes all required facts: it refers to the May 17 meeting at 11:00 A.M., names the attendees Kenneth Lay, Marty Sunde, and Bob Williams, and clearly asks where they should wait or go when they arrive. It matches the intent accurately and naturally.
The email is concise, polite, and professional throughout. It directly and respectfully asks for the exact waiting instructions, matching the requested tone and context well.

The email includes a clear subject line, a professional greeting, a concise body that states the request and meeting details, and a proper sign-off with a signature. The structure is clean and well-formatted.

The email includes every required fact: it schedules a project review meeting next week, lists the three available slots (Tuesday 2–3 PM, Wednesday 10–11 AM, Thursday 1–2 PM), asks recipients to indicate their availability, and says the schedule should be finalized soon.

The email is professional and concise, with a collaborative tone that invites the project team to share availability and help finalize the schedule. It uses polite language (“Please let me know,” “Kindly indicate”) and stays focused on the meeting request without unnecessary detail.

The email has a clear subject line, a proper greeting ('Dear Project Team,'), a well-structured body with separated paragraphs and bullet points listing the proposed time slots, and a professional sign-off with the sender’s name and title. It is polished, concise, and easy to scan.

The email includes all required facts accurately: it references invoice 48217 due on April 30, 2026, states the outstanding balance is $3,450, and asks for an update on the expected payment date. It also matches the sender, recipient, and professional tone without adding unsupported claims.

The email is firm, polite, and professional throughout: it clearly follows up on the overdue invoice, states the due date and amount, and asks for the expected payment date without sounding harsh or informal.

The email includes a clear subject line, a proper greeting, a concise and professional body, and a distinct sign-off with signature details. It is well formatted and easy to scan, matching the highest structure level.

The email includes every required fact accurately: it states the original Tuesday 2:00 PM interview, explains there is a conflict on their side, offers Wednesday 9:00 AM and Thursday 3:00 PM as alternatives, identifies the Software Engineer position at BrightPath Software Solutions, notes the interview will last about 90 minutes, and asks the candidate to confirm which time works.

The email is professional, courteous, apologetic, and concise throughout. It clearly apologizes for the scheduling conflict, politely proposes two alternative time slots, and asks the candidate to confirm availability, matching the requested tone very closely.

The email includes a clear subject line, a proper greeting (“Dear Avery Johnson,”), well-separated body paragraphs, a concise sign-off (“Best regards,”), and a signature with the sender’s name and role. It is polished and professionally formatted.

The email includes the early-bird deadline of September 10, 2025, the conference dates of October 22–24, 2025, the $300 early-bird fee versus the $400 regular fee, and encourages recipients to register soon to save money. It also matches the North Shore Innovation Summit affiliation. The only missing key fact is the current date of September 5, 2025, which is not stated.

The email is consistently informative and professional, with a clear reminder about the deadline, dates, and pricing. It also has a mild upbeat tone in phrases like "we encourage you" and "We hope to see you there," which fits the requested upbeat-professional style.

The email includes a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, well-separated body paragraphs, and a professional sign-off. The structure is polished and easy to scan, with the reminder, dates, pricing, and call to action presented clearly.

The email includes all required facts accurately: it states the design review is expected to finish by Friday, 19 June, 2026, explains the delay was caused by missing stakeholder feedback, and says no change is needed to the final launch date yet.

The email is transparent about the delay and its cause, uses a calm and reassuring tone, and clearly states that the final launch date does not need to change yet. It consistently provides a status update without sounding alarmist.

The email includes a clear subject, a distinct greeting, two well-formed body paragraphs, and a proper sign-off. It is polished and easy to scan, with only minor placeholders for recipient and sender names.

The email includes all required facts accurately: it states the meeting was about Q3 roadmap priorities, says the attached notes summarize the key decisions and action items, and asks recipients to send corrections by end of day Thursday. It also matches the sender and recipient context appropriately.

The email is clear, organized, and professional throughout: it politely shares the attached notes, summarizes the meeting purpose, and gives a direct correction deadline. The tone consistently matches the requested professional style with no noticeable mismatches.

The email is well structured and professional: it includes a clear subject, distinct greeting, a concise body in separate paragraphs, and a proper sign-off with sender name and affiliation. It directly covers the meeting notes, summarizes their purpose, and requests corrections by end of day Thursday.

The email includes all required facts accurately: it says the cleanup is on Saturday from 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM, asks volunteers to bring gloves and wear closed-toe shoes, and states that lunch and water will be provided. It also matches the intended inviting, appreciative tone and identifies the Community Outreach Organizer and Riverside Neighborhood Association.

The email is consistently friendly, encouraging, and appreciative: it warmly invites volunteers, says their help would make a meaningful difference, thanks them for considering the opportunity, and closes positively. The tone matches the requested community-oriented appreciation throughout.

The email includes a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, two well-separated body paragraphs, and a proper sign-off with sender affiliation. The structure is polished, professional, and easy to scan.

The email includes every required fact accurately: visitors must check in at the front desk, a government-issued photo ID is required for entry, and the new policy starts on Monday. The tone is formal and informative.

The email is consistently formal and informative, using polite business language such as "Please be advised," "Beginning Monday," and "Thank you for your attention." It clearly announces the policy update and presents the required details in a professional tone throughout.

The email includes a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, well-separated body paragraphs, and a professional sign-off. It is polished, concise, and formatted in a standard professional email structure.

The email includes all required facts: maintenance is scheduled for Sunday from 1:00 AM to 3:00 AM, the portal may be unavailable during that window, and service is expected to resume immediately after maintenance. The tone is brief, reassuring, and professional.

The email is brief, professional, and reassuring throughout. It clearly conveys the temporary outage, uses calm language such as "please be advised" and "thank you for your understanding and patience," and matches the requested tone closely.

The email is polished and professionally structured: it includes a clear subject, a distinct greeting, two well-separated body paragraphs covering the maintenance window and expected service resumption, and a proper sign-off. It is concise and easy to scan.
